seoTitle: How to Flip a Frame in Canva Easily
metaDescription: Learn how to flip a frame in Canva horizontally or vertically to adjust composition and improve the visual flow of your designs.
intro: Flipping a frame changes the direction an image or shape faces, which can improve the balance of a layout or make a design feel less repetitive. This guide covers flipping frame elements in Canva both horizontally and vertically.

steps.heading: Select the frame you want to flip
steps.body: Click on the frame element (with or without an image inside it) to bring up its editing toolbar.

steps.heading: Open the flip options
steps.body: Click the Flip button in the toolbar, or find it under the element's More options menu.

steps.heading: Choose horizontal or vertical flip
steps.body: Select 'Flip horizontal' to mirror the frame left-right, or 'Flip vertical' to mirror it top-bottom.

steps.heading: Check the image inside the frame
steps.body: Confirm the photo inside still looks correct after flipping, since text or logos in the image will also mirror.

steps.heading: Adjust positioning if needed
steps.body: Reposition the flipped frame within your layout to keep the overall composition balanced.

faq.question: Does flipping a frame also flip the image inside it?
faq.answer: Yes, the image inside moves with the frame, so check for any text or logos that might end up mirrored.

faq.question: Can I flip a frame back to its original direction?
faq.answer: Yes, simply click the flip option again or use undo to reverse the change.

faq.question: Is flipping a frame different from flipping a regular image?
faq.answer: The action works the same way, but frames also keep any crop and shape settings intact after the flip.

faq.question: Is this feature available for free?
faq.answer: Yes, flipping elements is a standard editing tool available to all Canva users.
